questionHtml: Calculate the hydroxide ion concentration in saturated aqueous hydrogen sulfide.

meta.title: Calculate the hydroxide ion concentration in saturated aqueous hydrogen sulfide.
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 3. What are the mechanisms of chemical change?

Reactivity 3.1.5—The ion product constant of water, Kw, shows an inverse relationship between [H+] and [OH–]. Kw = [H+] [OH–]. Recognize solutions as acidic, neutral and basic from the relative values of [H+] and [OH–].
